To top that off, destiny decides to make an entrance (in the form of a hard hit to a pretty skull) and Jungkook suffers amnesia, which only you seem to be able to handle. What will happen? title: My Cheating Amnesic Fiancé 2
summary: In which you (reader) and Jeon Jungkook are engaged due to an arrangement made by both of your parents. Or well, used to be. After finally resolving Jungkook's amnesia and your complicated relationship, one would think that everything was fine. But destiny isn't done messing with your life yet. Due to an incident where you confronted Jungkook's brother and your current fiancé about some lies that he had made, you found out about a horrible part of his life that you now feel obligated to help him with. But in order to do that, you cannot tell him about you and Jungkook. Additionally, Jungkook isn't done keeping secrets away from you - secrets that might destroy your already uncertain relationship permanently. Furthermore, your father's weak health is a constant worry on your mind, and as news outlets dig out more about your late grandfather's company's involvement with the former president, nothing in your already chaotic life will be stable for a long time. Will your relationship with Jungkook make it through all the hurdles and hindrances that destiny sets before you? Or are you two really not meant to be? A continuation of My Cheating Amnesic Fiancé. title: Little Bit Of Trouble
summary: This has been requested by multiple people, and I have never written a fanfic like this before so please bear with me. You close your eyes, afraid that when you open them again, you’ll be back in your hospital bed, forced back into the body riddled with disease, age and exhaustion. But nothing changes. The sunlight continues to play across your young skin. The breeze stirs wisps of your hair. You open your eyes to find him splayed out beside you, covers tossed to the side because he always got warmer than you did. Your boyfriend, your husband, the father of your children, and companion for the rest of his life. He died in your arms five years ago, a small wrinkled old man whose eyes sparkled with mischief and whose face crinkled with laughter in the same way it did all his eighty-one years. But he’s not eighty-one now. Today he’s twenty-one again. And he’s beautiful."

title: Royal Slave
summary: She went from a graceful princess to a pathetic slave... She went from having it all to being left with nothing... She went from living a happy life to that life getting destroyed to million of tiny pieces... And all this because of what?... Because she saved him back then, but now she curses herself for doing so... She was too kind for this cruel world, that left her with nothing but pain... A young girl who will have to face the hardships of the world, who will have to stand on her own two feet and fight with all her power in order to stay alive. She will meet danger while living next to her enemy, betrayal by her own people, but she will also come across an unexpected love and new hope. "You belong to me now, remember?..." he started taking slow steps towards me. I didn't dare to look at him. He leaned closer to my face and whispered in my ear, "Every part of this body and soul is mine... Only mine..." he said and his breath hit my skin sending chills down my spine. "I belong to no one" I glared at him. "Think that again, flower...".

summary: Sometimes I wonder if my power does more harm than good. I was born with the power to see how dangerous a person is on a scale of 1-10.  One being the least dangerous, mostly toddlers and young children, and ten being… deathly.  In all the years I’ve lived, I have discovered that most people are in the range of 4-6.  I have never come across a person with a 9 or 10.  They are extremely rare, and they must be one of the most dangerous humans in the world if they have a score of 10. title: They Don’t Love You Like I Love You
summary: Each time is like the first all over again. It starts perfectly innocent, with two friends catching up over late afternoon brunch.  You meet at your favourite restaurant - the place you had your first date, your second anniversary, your last birthday - and you order the same things you always do:  some kind of eggs benedict for him and something sweet for you. You ask about his mother’s wrist and his father’s golf game;  he tells you they’re both fine and that his brother’s dog might as well be their new grandchild.  He wants to know if you’ve gotten that promotion you so deserve yet, or if your sister’s finally decided on names for her soon-to-be-born twins.  No, you haven’t, and no, she haven’t. You offer little pieces of news like you do bits from your plate, trading a strawberry for one of his duck fat fried potatoes.  The conversation flows as easily as the coffee drains. It’s never uncomfortable or rushed or any of the million other things meetings with your exes tend to be. Which is probably why it’s so easy to end up in the position you are now, in the apartment you’d moved out of six months ago, draped across his chest with a book in your hand.

You push at his arm, urging him to go, but you'd have better luck trying to move one of the trees that surround you. The boy refuses to take his eyes off of you, and you freeze like a deer in the headlights. He opens his mouth as if to say something, but changes his mind and closes it again. Because there he walked on the sidewalk, dressed in a dark black hoodie and dark ripped jeans, his hair unkempt. He couldn't help but feel something actually good was going to happen tonight. On his journey to no place in particular, he happened to pass by a small restaurant, and inside sat a couple. They had been holding hands, smiles reaching to each side of their face. The scene only made his stomach churn while his smirk changed to a grim frown.

Your ultimate failure came in the form of one of Bangtan's members, Jeon JungKook. You had resisted JungKook for as long as you could, but in the end, you fell for him against your better judgment. You had broken the golden rule of detective work; forming attachments. Now, the only you could do was try to keep the two sides of your life - the SPD and Bangtan - from colliding and exposing your transgressions. Love, as they say, is a dangerous game.
